auditRules: Home button must ALWAYS be visible

issues.reason: Users can navigate to /project-builder but there is no home button or back navigation in the header. This violates the rule that home button must ALWAYS be visible. A child may get stuck on this page unable to return.
issues.fix: Add a home button (🏠) to the ProjectBuilderPage header that navigates back to /. Example: <button onClick={() => navigate('/')} className='text-3xl hover:scale-110'>🏠</button>
issues.currentImplementation: No header with navigation visible in ProjectBuilderPage component

issues.problem: SafeSearchPage uses 'Back Home' link instead of home button icon
issues.reason: While SafeSearchPage does have a home navigation via 'Back Home' Link component, it's styled as a text link button rather than the universally recognized home icon (🏠) used throughout the app. This creates visual inconsistency and children may not recognize it as the home button.
issues.fix: Replace the 'Back Home' text link with a home icon button matching YouTubeSearchView and VideoWatchPage: <button onClick={() => navigate('/')} className='text-3xl hover:scale-110' title='Back to home'>🏠</button>
issues.currentImplementation: <Link to='/' className='rounded-full bg-white px-4 py-2...'>Back Home</Link>

issues.problem: VideoWatchPage uses 'Back' arrow icon instead of home button
issues.reason: VideoWatchPage header only shows '← Back' button which takes users to the previous page, not home. If a child navigates deep into content (HomePage → Search → Video), they cannot jump directly home and must go back multiple times.
issues.fix: Add home button alongside back button: <button>🏠</button> and <button>← Back</button> in the header, or replace back with home button for direct home access.

issues.problem: Inconsistent navigation button styles across pages
issues.reason: Different pages use different home navigation approaches: YouTubeSearchView uses emoji button (🏠), SafeSearchPage uses text link, VideoWatchPage uses arrow button (←). This inconsistency can confuse children about how to navigate.
issues.fix: Standardize all navigation buttons across all pages. Every page should have: 1) Visible home button (🏠 emoji) 2) Back button (← arrow) if needed 3) Both consistently positioned in header 4) Matching Tailwind classes
issues.affectedPage: All pages (HomePage, YouTubeSearchView, VideoWatchPage, ProjectBuilderPage, SafeSearchPage)
issues.currentImplementation: Mixed: YouTubeSearchView has 🏠, SafeSearchPage has 'Back Home' Link, VideoWatchPage has ←

issues.problem: HomePage has no back button for context-switching
issues.reason: HomePage is the landing page and primary entry point, so no back button is acceptable. However, it should remain the root navigation hub that's easily accessible from anywhere.
issues.fix: No change required for HomePage itself, but ensure all other pages (URLs 2-5) have clear path back to HomePage. Verify Router structure maintains this invariant.
issues.currentImplementation: No navigation buttons (correct for home page)

issues.problem: Critical control visibility: Search bar not persistent across all content pages
issues.reason: YouTubeSearchView has a sticky search bar, but VideoWatchPage and ProjectBuilderPage may not. This means a child watching a video cannot search without going back. Search is a critical control for navigation.
issues.fix: Ensure search capability is always accessible. Options: 1) Add sticky search header to VideoWatchPage, 2) Add search icon in header that opens search overlay, 3) Provide breadcrumb navigation showing search path
issues.currentImplementation: VideoWatchPage and ProjectBuilderPage lack search bar in header

issues.problem: Project type selector in ProjectBuilderPage could be hidden during scrolling
issues.reason: ProjectBuilderPage project type selector is likely not sticky. A child scrolling through videos might lose visibility of how to change projects, reducing flexibility and potentially causing confusion.
issues.fix: Make the project type selector sticky (sticky positioning) or add a 'Change Project' button in the header that opens the selector in a modal.
issues.currentImplementation: Project type list renders at top but no sticky positioning specified

issues.problem: VideoWatchPage error state lacks home navigation
issues.reason: When VideoWatchPage encounters an error, it displays an error message with only a 'Go Back' button. If the error is persistent, children may be stuck in an error loop unable to navigate home.
issues.fix: Error state should have two buttons: 1) 'Go Back' with ← 2) 'Home' with 🏠 emoji. Provide explicit home escape route in error scenarios.
issues.currentImplementation: <button onClick={handleBack} className='btn-primary'>Go Back</button> (only back, no home option)

issues.problem: Diagnostics panel could obscure critical navigation
issues.reason: DiagnosticsPanel is fixed position bottom-right, dev-only feature toggled with Ctrl+Shift+P. If accidentally triggered, it could cover action buttons or important content in the bottom-right corner, hiding controls.
issues.fix: Ensure DiagnosticsPanel is z-50 (high z-index) so it layers above but doesn't hide critical UI. Also verify that it's truly dev-only (not accessible to children). Consider making it draggable if high priority.
issues.currentImplementation: z-50 applied, appears to be dev-only (Ctrl+Shift+P)

issues.problem: RouterContext dependency: Navigation relies entirely on React Router
issues.reason: All navigation uses React Router (navigate, Link, Routes). If router state breaks or a page loads outside the Router context, navigation buttons could fail silently, trapping users.
issues.fix: Add error boundaries around route transitions and ensure Router wraps entire app (already done in App.jsx). Consider adding manual window.history fallback: if navigate() fails, use window.history.back() or window.location.href
issues.currentImplementation: Router wraps all routes in App.jsx - good structure, but consider fallback navigation

summary.mostCriticalIssue: UX-001: ProjectBuilderPage lacks home/back navigation - children could be trapped on this page
quickWins: Add home button to ProjectBuilderPage header
Add home option to VideoWatchPage error state
Standardize navigation button appearance across all pages

priority_1_critical.changes: Add header with home button (🏠) and optional back button (←) to component top
In error state, add home button alongside 'Go Back' button

priority_2_important.changes: Create reusable NavigationHeader component with home + back buttons for consistency
Replace 'Back Home' Link with emoji home button matching YouTubeSearchView
Add home button to header alongside ← Back button

priority_3_nice_to_have.changes: Add sticky positioning to project type selector
Add error handling for navigate() calls with window.history.back() fallback
